examples.title: Basic Input Output Program
examples.description: A simple program demonstrating printf() and scanf() for reading and displaying user information
examples.explanation: This program demonstrates basic I/O operations: using scanf() with %s for strings (no & needed), %d for integers, %f for floats. The %.2f format prints salary with exactly 2 decimal places.

examples.title: Formatted Output with Width and Precision
examples.description: Demonstrates field width and precision specifiers in printf()
examples.explanation: This program shows formatting options: %wd sets field width, %.wp sets precision (decimal places). Negative width (%-wd) enables left-alignment. Combining both: %w.pf sets both width and precision.

examples.title: Temperature Conversion
examples.description: C program to convert temperature between Celsius and Fahrenheit using formatted I/O
examples.explanation: This program demonstrates multiple scanf() calls with %f for float and %d for integer choice. It uses %.2f in printf() to display temperatures with 2 decimal places. The if-else structure handles user selection.

examples.title: Character Input Output Functions
examples.description: Demonstrates getchar(), putchar() and string I/O functions gets() and puts()
examples.explanation: This program shows character I/O: getchar() reads one character, putchar() outputs one character. It demonstrates why fgets() should be used instead of the unsafe gets() function for string input. puts() automatically adds a newline.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is string length
